pattern: Breadth-first search over states of (current node, set of visited nodes) finds the fewest edges needed to cover every node, since each step adds one edge of uniform cost.
hints: The state you explore is not just where you are but also which nodes you have already touched.
Encode the visited set as a bitmask, so a state is the pair of current node and that bitmask.
Start a breadth-first search seeded from every node with only itself visited, expanding along edges and updating the bitmask, and stop when the bitmask marks all nodes visited.

edge_inputs.description: a star where the center must be revisited
denser graph with multiple short routes
two connected nodes need one edge
a path graph forces traversal end to end
a triangle, every node one hop away
two leaves off a path require backtracking

stress.description: random connected graphs built from a spanning tree plus extra edges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(max(1, size), 12))
    adj = [set() for _ in range(n)]
    for v in range(1, n):
        u = rng.randrange(v)
        adj[u].add(v)
        adj[v].add(u)
    extra = rng.randint(0, n)
    for _ in range(extra):
        a = rng.randrange(n)
        b = rng.randrange(n)
        if a != b:
            adj[a].add(b)
            adj[b].add(a)
    return [[sorted(adj[i]) for i in range(n)]]
stress.note: spanning tree guarantees connectivity; n capped at 12 keeps state space small
